title: phacelia purshii var. purshii
intro: Phacelia purshii var. purshii, also known as desert lavender or purple phacelia, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. Desert lavender can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as salves and creams, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in a carrier oil or vinegar.
